name: Log form submissions with Notion and Telegram in real time

parameters.formTitle: Log form submissions with Notion and Telegram in real time

parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'from' on incoming email.

parameters.text: =✅ Log form submissions with Notion and Telegram in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Email Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (From, Subject, Receivedat). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.from }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Prepare Approval Packet · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Telegram

### Request Approval via Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Hold for Decision · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Execute Approved Action (API) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Execute Approved Action (API)

POST https://api.example.com/v1/emails — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per email (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Email Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Telegram · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Email Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Log form submissions with Notion and Telegram in real time

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Log form submissions with Notion and Telegram in real time. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual auto-responders is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Email Form Submission → Required Fields Present? → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Telegram → Hold for Decision → Approved? → Execute Approved Action (API) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating auto-responders in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The workflow pauses on a Wait node configured to resume via webhook — n8n's actual human-in-the-loop primitive, not a polling loop. The resume URL is embedded directly in the approval message so approving is one click, and the paused execution burns no compute for however long the decision takes.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a timeout to the Wait node so unanswered approvals auto-decline after N hours.
2. Change the approval channel and confirm the resume link still works.
3. Add a third outcome (e.g. 'needs changes') alongside approve/decline.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your auto-responders process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
